---
title: "Mapping Workflow"
author: "AY"
---

This page documents the unified mapping workflow (`STAR`, `bwa`, `bowtie2`) driven via `./scripts/bcfngs`.

## Overview

- One workflow file: `workflow/mapping.smk`
- One CLI entrypoint:
  - `./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan`
  - `./scripts/bcfngs mapping run`
- Default mapper: `star`
- Default read mode: paired-end
- Default bigWig conversion: `bamCoverage --normalizeUsing CPM --binSize 10`

Output layout:

```text
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/<mapper>/<sample>.bam
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/<mapper>/<sample>.bam.bai
<outdir>/<project>/<organism>/<mapper>/bigwig/<sample>.bw
```

For `STAR`, the workflow also keeps:

- `<sample>.counts.tab`
- `<sample>.SJ.out.tab`

## Required configuration

Update `configs/run.yaml`:

```yaml
project: "P000"
path: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/"
outdir: "/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping"
organism: "Caenorhabditis_elegans"
threads: 16
ram_bytes: 168632718037
```

`organism` must be a single organism key from `configs/genomes.yaml`.

## FASTQ pattern auto-detection

The workflow detects input naming in this fixed order:

1. `*_R1.fastq.gz`
2. `*_R1_001.fastq.gz`
3. `*_L1_R1.fastq.gz`
4. `*.conc.R1.fastq.gz`

In paired-end mode, the corresponding `R2` file must exist.

## Basic usage

Dry-run first:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
```

Run:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run
```

Select mapper(s):

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --mapper star,bwa,bowtie2
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --mapper bwa
```

Read mode:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --single-end
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --paired-end
```

Override config values for a single run:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --project P152 \
  --organism Drosophila_melanogaster \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P152/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
```

Keep intermediary files (default is cleanup):

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --keep-intermediary-files
```

Tune bigWig options:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --bw-normalization CPM --bw-bin-size 10
```

## SLURM execution

Run mapping on SLURM:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --executor slurm --jobs 20
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --executor slurm --jobs 20
```

Defaults:

- Partition: `p.hpcl8`
- Mail user: `yeroslaviz@biochem.mpg.de`
- Mail type: `ALL`
- Time: `04:00:00`

Key SLURM overrides:

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run \
  --executor slurm \
  --slurm-partition p.hpcl8 \
  --slurm-time 04:00:00 \
  --slurm-cpus-per-task 20 \
  --slurm-star-mem-mb 64000 \
  --slurm-aligner-mem-mb 32000
```

The wrapper sets per-rule resources so lightweight rules (indexing and bamCoverage) do not request mapper-level memory.

## Additional Examples Moved From `snakemake.Rmd`

These command blocks were moved here to keep mapping examples in one place.

```bash
# default (paired-end, STAR)
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run

# one mapper
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper bwa

# all mappers
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run --mapper star,bwa,bowtie2
```

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan \
  --project P152 \
  --organism Drosophila_melanogaster \
  --fastq-dir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P152/conc.fastq \
  --outdir /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/mapping
```

```bash
./scripts/bcfngs mapping plan --executor slurm --jobs 20
./scripts/bcfngs mapping run  --executor slurm --jobs 20
```

Legacy STAR-only direct Snakemake examples:

```bash
cd /fs/pool/pool-bcfngs/fastq_files/P000/P000_Testrun/conc.fastq/

# for single-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.SingleEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
# for paired-end samples
snakemake -nps /path/to/repo/Star.MappingQuant.PairedEndFastq.Snakefile -j 50
```

Flag meanings for the legacy command style:

- `-n`: dry-run
- `-p`: print shell commands
- `-s`: snakefile path
- `-j`: number of cores/jobs
